column1: Proto-Indo-European (PIE) culture (c. 4000–2500 BCE, Pontic-Caspian Steppe) is not urban but is the linguistic ancestor of most Eurasian languages.
This table includes both urban civilizations and influential non-urban cultures (e.g., Scythians, PIE, Polynesians).
Dates are approximate and often overlap due to regional variation and cultural continuity.
For brevity, only representative civilizations are listed; many more minor cultures existed regionally.
This table provides a global perspective on the rise and spread of civilization, from the earliest farming societies to the great empires of the pre-modern world123678.
